enrichment.short_description: Courier is a multichannel notification API that lets developers send email, SMS, push, Slack, Teams, and in-app messages through one integration.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan (10,000 sends/month); Business plan pay-as-you-go from $0.005 per send
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Courier is a multichannel notification API for email, SMS, push, chat, and in-app messages, with journeys, templates, and a free 10K-send tier.

features.title: Omnichannel routing
features.description: Send email, SMS, push, Slack, Microsoft Teams, and in-app notifications through a single API instead of integrating each provider separately.

features.title: Customer journeys
features.description: Multi-step automated workflows triggered by user actions or data events, with personalization and branching logic.

features.title: Visual design studio
features.description: No-code template editor for building and previewing notification content across channels.

features.title: Notification inbox
features.description: Embeddable in-app notification center component for adding a bell/inbox UI to a product.

features.title: Preference center
features.description: Built-in user preference management so recipients can control which notifications and channels they receive.

features.title: 50+ provider integrations
features.description: Pre-built integrations with providers such as SendGrid, Twilio, Firebase Cloud Messaging, and Apple Push Notification service.

features.title: Delivery observability
features.description: End-to-end delivery tracking and engagement analytics for every notification sent.

features.title: Multi-tenant support
features.description: Tenant-level routing and configuration for SaaS platforms serving multiple customers from one account.

pros: Single API covers email, SMS, push, chat, and in-app channels instead of juggling separate provider SDKs
Free tier includes 10,000 sends per month with no credit card required
Visual, no-code template and journey builders reduce engineering time for notification changes
Detailed delivery logs and analytics for debugging notification issues
Cited in production use by companies such as Twilio and Nav in Courier's published case studies
cons: Usage-based Business plan pricing ($0.005/send) can scale unpredictably at high volume
Enterprise features like SSO, RBAC, and EU data residency require custom/Enterprise pricing
Requires integrating your own downstream provider accounts (e.g., Twilio, SendGrid) for many channels

faqs.question: Does Courier have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Developer plan is free and includes 10,000 sends per month with access to journeys, broadcasts, and SDKs.

faqs.question: What channels does Courier support?
faqs.answer: Courier routes notifications across email, SMS, push notifications, Slack, Microsoft Teams, and in-app messaging.

faqs.question: How is paid usage billed?
faqs.answer: The Business plan is pay-as-you-go at $0.005 per send, on top of the features included in the free Developer plan.

faqs.question: Can I build a notification inbox with Courier?
faqs.answer: Yes, Courier provides an embeddable notification center/inbox component for in-app notifications.

use_cases.title: Product notification infrastructure
use_cases.description: Replace custom-built email/SMS/push integrations with a single API and template layer.

use_cases.title: Customer engagement journeys
use_cases.description: Trigger multi-step, personalized messaging sequences based on user behavior or backend events.

use_cases.title: In-app notification center
use_cases.description: Add a bell-icon notification inbox to a web or mobile app using Courier's prebuilt component.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Courier?
paragraphs: Courier is a notification infrastructure platform that gives developers a single API to send messages across email, SMS, push, Slack, Microsoft Teams, and in-app channels, instead of integrating each provider individually.
The platform includes a visual template editor, multi-step customer journeys, a notification preference center, and delivery observability, plus a free tier for smaller-volume senders.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Courier is for
paragraphs: Courier is aimed at engineering and product teams that need to send transactional or lifecycle notifications across multiple channels and want centralized templates, routing, and delivery tracking rather than building that infrastructure in-house.

enrichment.short_description: SuprSend is a notification infrastructure API for building email, SMS, push, WhatsApp, Slack, and in-app notification workflows from one platform.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan (10,000 notifications/month); paid plans from $110/month ($100/month billed annually)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SuprSend is an omnichannel notification API with workflows, in-app inbox, and batching, offering a free 10K/month tier and paid plans from $110/month.

features.title: Workflows
features.description: No-code workflow builder for constructing notification logic, with AI assistance for building flows.

features.title: Templates
features.description: WYSIWYG, multi-channel template editor with versioning and A/B testing support.

features.title: Smart channel routing
features.description: Automatically routes notifications to the best channel to help reduce user fatigue.

features.title: Batching & digest
features.description: Aggregates multiple events into a single digested notification instead of sending each one separately.

features.title: In-app inbox
features.description: Socket-based, embeddable in-app notification center.

features.title: Preferences center
features.description: Granular per-channel opt-in/opt-out controls for end users.

features.title: Multi-tenancy
features.description: Per-tenant customization and routing for SaaS platforms, available on the Enterprise plan.

features.title: MCP server support
features.description: Native MCP server integration for connecting AI assistants such as Claude, Cursor, and Windsurf to notification workflows.

pros: Free plan includes 10,000 notifications/month across unlimited channels with unlimited team members
Supports a wide channel set including WhatsApp, Slack, and Microsoft Teams alongside email/SMS/push
Enterprise plan offers self-hosted and Bring Your Own Cloud deployment options for compliance-sensitive teams
SOC 2 Type 2 and HIPAA compliance stated for enterprise customers
cons: In-app feed carries SuprSend branding until the Essentials plan or higher
Batching/digest and user preferences are gated behind the Business plan, not included in Free or Essentials
Per-notification overage pricing ($2-$5 per 1,000) can add up for high-volume senders

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, SuprSend's Free plan includes 10,000 notifications per month with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What channels does SuprSend support?
faqs.answer: Email, SMS, mobile push, web push, WhatsApp, Slack, Microsoft Teams, and an in-app inbox.

faqs.question: Can SuprSend be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: The Enterprise plan offers self-hosted and Bring Your Own Cloud deployment options.

faqs.question: Does SuprSend support AI workflows?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers an Agent Node for AI-driven runtime decisions and MCP server support for AI assistants like Claude and Cursor.

use_cases.title: Multi-channel product notifications
use_cases.description: Send transactional and product notifications across email, push, and in-app channels from a single workflow.

use_cases.title: Notification digesting
use_cases.description: Batch multiple triggered events into a single digest to reduce notification fatigue.

use_cases.title: Enterprise self-hosted messaging
use_cases.description: Deploy SuprSend in a private cloud or self-hosted environment to meet compliance requirements.

features: Sync logs to Segment or data warehouse

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SuprSend?
paragraphs: SuprSend is a notification infrastructure platform that provides a unified API, workflow builder, and in-app inbox for sending product and marketing notifications across email, SMS, push, WhatsApp, Slack, and Microsoft Teams.
It includes features such as batching/digest, timezone-aware sending, user preference centers, and MCP server support for connecting AI assistants to notification workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SuprSend is for
paragraphs: SuprSend targets engineering and product teams that need to unify product and marketing notification sending across many channels, including teams with compliance needs that require self-hosted or Bring Your Own Cloud deployment.

enrichment.short_description: MagicBell is a notification API and inbox component for sending email, push, web push, SMS, and Slack messages with a free 1,000-delivery tier.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan (1,000 deliveries/month); paid plans from $249/month
enrichment.seo_meta_description: MagicBell is a multichannel notification API with a prebuilt inbox and delivery tracking, offering a free 1,000-delivery/month tier and plans from $249/mo.

features.title: Unified notification API
features.description: Single API to send mobile push, web push, email, SMS, and Slack notifications.

features.title: Notification inbox component
features.description: Prebuilt, embeddable in-app notification center UI.

features.title: Delivery tracking
features.description: Full delivery event logging with raw payload inspection for debugging.

features.title: Preference management
features.description: Built-in user notification preference controls.

features.title: Smart Delivery / fallback rules
features.description: Configurable channel prioritization and fallback rules if a notification fails on one channel.

features.title: Broad provider integrations
features.description: Integrates with APNs, FCM, Expo, Twilio, SendGrid, Mailgun, Amazon SES, SMTP, Slack, and Microsoft Teams.

pros: Free Builder plan includes 1,000 deliveries/month with access to all integrations and unlimited team members
Prebuilt notification inbox component speeds up adding in-app notifications to a product
Detailed delivery logs with raw payload inspection aid debugging
States over 1 billion notifications delivered and 99.99% uptime across 10,000+ active projects
cons: Startup plan jumps to $249/month for 50,000 deliveries, a large step up from the free tier
Additional projects beyond the included 5 cost $99 each on the Startup plan
Deliveries are counted per channel, so a single multi-channel notification counts as multiple deliveries

faqs.question: Does MagicBell have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Builder plan is free and includes 1,000 deliveries per month with access to all integrations.

faqs.question: What channels does MagicBell support?
faqs.answer: Mobile push, web push, email, SMS, Slack, and Microsoft Teams, plus an in-app notification inbox.

faqs.question: How are deliveries counted?
faqs.answer: A delivery is counted per channel, so one notification sent to multiple channels counts as multiple deliveries.

faqs.question: What does the Startup plan include?
faqs.answer: 50,000 deliveries per month for $249/month, with $0.0025 per additional delivery and 5 projects included.

use_cases.title: In-app notification inbox
use_cases.description: Add a prebuilt bell-icon notification center to a web or mobile app.

use_cases.title: Multichannel delivery with fallback
use_cases.description: Send notifications with configured fallback rules across push, email, and SMS if one channel fails.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is MagicBell?
paragraphs: MagicBell is a notification infrastructure service that lets developers send mobile push, web push, email, SMS, and Slack notifications through a single API and manage them with a prebuilt notification inbox component.
It provides delivery tracking, user preference management, and channel fallback rules, with a free tier covering 1,000 deliveries per month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who MagicBell is for
paragraphs: MagicBell is built for product and engineering teams that want to add a multichannel notification system and in-app inbox to their application without building the delivery and preference infrastructure themselves.

enrichment.short_description: Engagespot is a notification infrastructure API offering unified email, SMS, push, Slack, Discord and WhatsApp messaging with a free 10,000-trigger tier.
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan (10,000 event triggers/month); paid plans from $250/month
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Engagespot is a notification API with an in-app inbox, workflow builder, and broad SDK support, offering a free 10K-trigger tier and plans from $250/mo.

features.title: Unified notification API
features.description: Single API for email, SMS, push, webhooks, Slack, Discord, and WhatsApp.

features.title: In-app notification inbox
features.description: Prebuilt, customizable notification inbox UI components.

features.title: Template editor
features.description: Editor for managing multi-channel notification content.

features.title: Workflow builder
features.description: Visual builder for multi-step notification logic.

features.title: Preference management
features.description: User-level preference controls for channels and notification types.

features.title: CLI for deployments
features.description: Command-line tool for version-controlling and deploying notification templates and workflows to production.

features.title: Broad SDK coverage
features.description: SDKs for React, Angular, Vue, Flutter, React Native, Node.js, Laravel, Symfony, Go, and Rust.

pros: Free Launch plan includes 10,000 event triggers/month with unlimited channels and workflows
Broad SDK support across major frontend, mobile, and backend frameworks
Enterprise plan offers private cloud or on-premises deployment for compliance needs
States 850M+ notifications delivered and 3.7B+ API requests handled monthly at 99.98% uptime
cons: Engagespot branding remains on the notification inbox until the Growth plan or higher
Growth plan starts at $250/month, a significant jump from the free tier
Enterprise pricing starts at $2,500/month for custom SLAs and dedicated support

faqs.question: Does Engagespot have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Launch plan is free forever and includes 10,000 event triggers per month with unlimited channels and workflows.

faqs.question: What channels does Engagespot support?
faqs.answer: In-app, email, web push, mobile push, SMS, Slack, Discord, WhatsApp, and webhooks.

faqs.question: Can Engagespot be deployed on-premises?
faqs.answer: The Enterprise plan offers private cloud or on-premises deployment options.

faqs.question: What frameworks does Engagespot have SDKs for?
faqs.answer: React, Angular, Vue, Flutter, React Native, Node.js, Laravel, Symfony, Go, and Rust.

use_cases.title: Multi-channel product notifications
use_cases.description: Send in-app, email, push, and messaging-app notifications from a single workflow using framework-specific SDKs.
use_cases.audience: Developers across web and mobile stacks

use_cases.title: Enterprise on-premises notifications
use_cases.description: Run Engagespot in a private cloud or on-premises for organizations with strict data residency needs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Engagespot?
paragraphs: Engagespot is a notification infrastructure platform giving developers a unified API and prebuilt in-app inbox for sending notifications across email, SMS, push, Slack, Discord, and WhatsApp.
It offers a workflow builder, template editor, and SDKs for major web and mobile frameworks, plus a free tier of 10,000 event triggers per month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Engagespot is for
paragraphs: Engagespot is aimed at development teams that need a multi-channel notification system with framework-specific SDKs, and at larger organizations that need on-premises or private-cloud deployment for compliance.

enrichment.short_description: Gotify is a free, open-source, self-hosted server for sending and receiving push messages via REST API and WebSocket, with an Android app.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Gotify is an open-source, self-hosted push notification server (MIT license) with a REST API, WebSocket delivery, web UI, and Android app.

features.title: REST API messaging
features.description: Send messages to the server via a simple REST API.

features.title: WebSocket delivery
features.description: Clients receive messages in real time over WebSocket connections.

features.title: User/app/client management
features.description: Admin controls for managing users, client devices, and registered applications.

features.title: Web UI
features.description: Built-in web interface for server administration and message management.

features.title: Android client
features.description: Dedicated Android app available on Google Play and F-Droid for receiving push notifications.

features.title: Plugin support
features.description: Server is extensible via plugins.

features.title: CLI tool
features.description: Command-line client for pushing messages, in addition to using any HTTP client.

pros: Fully free and open source under the MIT license
Lightweight, self-hosted alternative to commercial push notification services
Simple REST API makes it easy to integrate with scripts and other tools
Available as an Android app via both Google Play and F-Droid
cons: No official iOS or desktop client mentioned
Requires self-hosting and server maintenance since there's no managed cloud option
Feature set is intentionally minimal compared to full-featured notification platforms

faqs.question: Is Gotify free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Gotify is fully open source under the MIT license and free to self-host.

faqs.question: How do I send messages with Gotify?
faqs.answer: Messages are sent via a REST API call to your self-hosted server.

faqs.question: Does Gotify have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, an Android client is available on Google Play and F-Droid.

faqs.question: Is there a hosted version of Gotify?
faqs.answer: No, Gotify is self-hosted only; there is no official managed cloud service.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted alerting for scripts and servers
use_cases.description: Send push alerts from cron jobs, monitoring scripts, or CI pipelines to a phone or desktop.

use_cases.title: Lightweight app notification backend
use_cases.description: Run a self-hosted message server for small apps needing simple push notifications.
use_cases.audience: Developers wanting a self-hosted alternative to commercial push services

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Gotify?
paragraphs: Gotify is an open-source, self-hosted server for sending and receiving messages in real time. Applications and scripts push messages to the server via a REST API, and connected clients receive them instantly over WebSocket.
It includes a web UI for administering users, applications, and clients, plus a native Android app, and is released under the MIT license.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Gotify is for
paragraphs: Gotify suits developers, sysadmins, and homelab users who want a simple, self-hosted push notification server for scripts, monitoring tools, and small applications without relying on a third-party service.

enrichment.short_description: ntfy is an open-source HTTP pub-sub notification service, usable free on the hosted ntfy.sh or self-hosted, with paid plans for higher limits.
enrichment.starting_price: Free hosted tier available; paid plans from $5/month billed annually ($6/month monthly)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ntfy is an open-source pub-sub push notification service via HTTP, free on ntfy.sh or self-hosted, with paid plans from $5/month for higher limits.

features.title: HTTP pub-sub messaging
features.description: Send notifications by making a simple PUT/POST HTTP request to a topic URL; no signup required.

features.title: Dynamic topics
features.description: Topics are created on the fly just by publishing or subscribing to a URL path.

features.title: Rich message options
features.description: Support for priorities, action buttons, tags/emojis, and file attachments such as images, video, and documents.

features.title: Multi-platform apps
features.description: Native apps for iOS and Android, plus a web app and CLI support.

features.title: Self-hostable server
features.description: Open-source server that can be self-hosted instead of using the public ntfy.sh service.

features.title: Third-party integrations
features.description: Used with tools such as GitHub Actions, Home Assistant, Sonarr, and Radarr for alerting.

pros: Free to use on the public ntfy.sh service without signup or fees
Fully open source (dual Apache 2.0 / GPLv2 licensed) and self-hostable
Native iOS, Android, web, and CLI clients
Simple integration via plain HTTP requests, compatible with curl and most scripting languages
cons: Free-tier topics act like public passwords and aren't reserved, so anyone who guesses the topic name can subscribe or publish
Paid plans are required to reserve topic names or increase daily message/attachment limits
Business plan tops out at 50,000 daily messages before requiring a custom arrangement

faqs.question: Is ntfy free?
faqs.answer: Yes, ntfy.sh can be used for free without signing up, and the server software is free and open source to self-host.

faqs.question: What are ntfy's paid plans?
faqs.answer: Supporter ($5-6/mo), Pro ($10-12/mo), and Business ($20-25/mo) plans add reserved topics, higher message limits, and larger attachments.

faqs.question: Can I self-host ntfy?
faqs.answer: Yes, the ntfy server is open source (Apache 2.0 / GPLv2) and documented for self-hosting.

faqs.question: What platforms does ntfy support?
faqs.answer: iOS, Android, web browsers, and any HTTP client such as curl.

use_cases.title: Script and server alerting
use_cases.description: Send notifications from shell scripts, cron jobs, or CI/CD pipelines using a single HTTP request.

use_cases.title: Home automation alerts
use_cases.description: Integrate with tools like Home Assistant, Sonarr, or Radarr to push status alerts to a phone.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted push notifications
use_cases.description: Run a private ntfy server for internal tooling without depending on a third-party service.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ntfy?
paragraphs: ntfy is an open-source, HTTP-based publish-subscribe notification service. Users publish messages by sending a PUT or POST request to a topic URL, and any subscriber to that topic receives the notification instantly.

It's free to use on the public ntfy.sh service without signup, dual-licensed under Apache 2.0 and GPLv2, and can be self-hosted; paid Supporter, Pro, and Business plans add reserved topic names and higher limits.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ntfy is for
paragraphs: ntfy is built for developers, sysadmins, and homelab users who want to send push notifications from scripts, servers, or home automation tools without building notification infrastructure from scratch.

enrichment.short_description: Listmonk is a free, open-source, self-hosted newsletter and mailing list manager delivered as a single Go binary with a Postgres backend.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Listmonk is a free, open-source, self-hosted email newsletter and mailing list manager built as a single Go binary, requiring PostgreSQL.

features.title: Single-binary deployment
features.description: Ships as a single Go binary (or Docker image) with no external runtime dependencies beyond PostgreSQL.

features.title: High-throughput sending
features.description: Multi-threaded SMTP queues with rate limiting designed to handle mailing lists with millions of subscribers.

features.title: Campaign analytics
features.description: Built-in tracking for opens, clicks, bounces, and link performance.

features.title: Visual and template-based email builder
features.description: Drag-and-drop visual builder alongside Go-template-based dynamic email templating.

features.title: Transactional API
features.description: API for sending one-off transactional messages in addition to bulk campaigns.

features.title: Extensible messengers
features.description: HTTP webhook-based messenger interfaces for extending delivery to channels beyond email.

features.title: OIDC SSO
features.description: Single sign-on support for admin authentication.

pros: Completely free and open source (AGPLv3) with no paid tier
Single binary is simple to deploy and self-host
Designed to scale to millions of subscribers with multi-threaded sending queues
Built-in analytics reduce the need for separate tracking tools
cons: Requires self-hosting and a PostgreSQL database; no official managed cloud version from the maintainers
No built-in SMTP sending service — you must supply your own SMTP/mail provider
AGPLv3 license may impose obligations for organizations building derivative hosted services

faqs.question: Is Listmonk free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's fully open source under the AGPLv3 license with no paid tier.

faqs.question: What database does Listmonk require?
faqs.answer: PostgreSQL.

faqs.question: Can Listmonk send transactional emails?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes a transactional messaging API in addition to bulk campaigns.

faqs.question: Is there a hosted version of Listmonk?
faqs.answer: The core project is self-hosted, though third-party hosts like Railway and Northflank offer one-click deployments.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted newsletter sending
use_cases.description: Run your own mailing list infrastructure instead of paying per-subscriber SaaS fees.

use_cases.title: Large-scale opt-in mailing lists
use_cases.description: Manage and send to mailing lists with millions of subscribers using multi-threaded SMTP queues.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Listmonk?
paragraphs: Listmonk is a self-hosted, open-source newsletter and mailing list manager distributed as a single Go binary that requires only a PostgreSQL database to run.

It supports high-throughput multi-threaded email sending, built-in campaign analytics, drag-and-drop and template-based email building, and a transactional messaging API, all under the AGPLv3 license.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Listmonk is for
paragraphs: Listmonk is aimed at developers, publishers, and organizations that want to run their own newsletter infrastructure rather than pay per-subscriber SaaS pricing, and that are comfortable self-hosting a Go binary with PostgreSQL.

enrichment.short_description: Mautic is an open-source marketing automation platform for email, landing pages, and lead nurturing, self-hostable for free or available as managed hosting.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (self-hosted community edition); official managed hosting via Dropsolid from €247.50/month
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mautic is a free, open-source marketing automation platform for email, landing pages, and lead nurturing, self-hostable or available as managed hosting.

features.title: Campaign builder
features.description: Drag-and-drop visual campaign builder for multi-step marketing automation.

features.title: Email & landing page builder
features.description: Mobile-optimized email and landing page builder.

features.title: Lead scoring & nurturing
features.description: Score and segment leads based on behavior and data for nurture campaigns.

features.title: Omnichannel messaging
features.description: Supports email, SMS, WhatsApp, and notification channels.

features.title: REST API
features.description: Full REST API for integrations with other systems.

features.title: Plugin ecosystem
features.description: Extensible via a plugin and integration ecosystem.

features.title: Self-hosted data ownership
features.description: Runs on your own infrastructure, keeping campaign and contact data under your control.

pros: Core software is free and open source, with no vendor lock-in
Self-hosting gives full control and ownership of marketing data
Supports multi-channel campaigns (email, SMS, WhatsApp) beyond just email
Large community, described as used by 200,000+ companies with 40,000+ companies participating in the community
cons: Official managed/cloud hosting (via partner Dropsolid) starts at €247.50/month, a significant cost versus self-hosting
Self-hosted deployments require you to manage servers, updates, and email deliverability yourself
Higher-volume managed plans (Professional at €1,237/month) are priced for larger organizations, not solo users

faqs.question: Is Mautic free?
faqs.answer: The core Mautic software is free and open source; official managed hosting plans are paid.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Mautic?
faqs.answer: Yes, self-hosting on your own infrastructure is a primary supported deployment method.

faqs.question: What channels does Mautic support?
faqs.answer: Email, SMS, WhatsApp, and other notification channels alongside landing pages.

faqs.question: Is there official managed hosting?
faqs.answer: Yes, Managed Mautic is offered through the partner Dropsolid, starting at €247.50/month billed annually.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted marketing automation
use_cases.description: Run lead nurturing, email campaigns, and landing pages on your own infrastructure without SaaS fees.

use_cases.title: Multi-channel lead nurturing
use_cases.description: Score and segment leads, then nurture them across email, SMS, and WhatsApp.

use_cases.title: Managed marketing automation
use_cases.description: Use officially managed Mautic hosting instead of self-hosting for teams that want a hands-off deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mautic?
paragraphs: Mautic is an open-source marketing automation platform for building campaigns, nurturing leads, and running email, landing page, and multi-channel marketing without vendor lock-in.
The core software is free to self-host, while officially managed Mautic hosting is available through the partner Dropsolid for teams that want a hands-off deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mautic is for
paragraphs: Mautic suits marketing teams and developers who want an open-source, self-hostable alternative to commercial marketing automation SaaS, particularly those prioritizing data ownership and privacy.

enrichment.short_description: Keila is an open-source email newsletter platform, free to self-host (AGPLv3) or available as EU-hosted Keila Cloud starting at €8/month.
enrichment.starting_price: €8/month (Keila Cloud XS plan); free to self-host
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Keila is an open-source (AGPLv3) email newsletter platform, free to self-host or available as EU-hosted Keila Cloud from €8/month.

features.title: Visual & Markdown editors
features.description: Block-based visual editor, Markdown support, MJML customization, and plain-text sending.

features.title: Segmentation
features.description: Target contacts based on data and behavior.

features.title: Form builder
features.description: Customizable signup forms with bot protection, including captcha and double opt-in.

features.title: Personalization
features.description: Liquid templating language for dynamic, personalized content.

features.title: API
features.description: REST API for integrations and workflow automation.

features.title: Transactional email
features.description: Send one-off transactional messages via the API in addition to bulk campaigns.

features.title: Privacy-first analytics
features.description: Campaign analytics with the option to disable tracking entirely.

pros: Fully open source (AGPLv3) with no proprietary premium-only features
Free to self-host indefinitely via official Docker images
Keila Cloud is hosted on EU (Germany/France) infrastructure for GDPR-focused users
Tracking and analytics can be disabled for privacy-conscious senders
cons: Keila Cloud has no free plan — the cheapest hosted tier is €8/month for 2,000 emails
Self-hosting requires running and maintaining your own Docker deployment
Cloud pricing is in euros, which may add currency-conversion friction for non-EU customers

faqs.question: Is Keila free?
faqs.answer: The self-hosted, open-source version is free (AGPLv3); the managed Keila Cloud service is paid, starting at €8/month.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Keila?
faqs.answer: Yes, official Docker images are provided for self-hosting.

faqs.question: Where is Keila Cloud hosted?
faqs.answer: On European infrastructure in Germany and France.

faqs.question: Does Keila support transactional email?
faqs.answer: Yes, via its API in addition to bulk newsletter campaigns.

use_cases.title: EU-hosted newsletter sending
use_cases.description: Send newsletters via Keila Cloud on EU infrastructure for GDPR-focused compliance.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted email newsletters
use_cases.description: Run your own open-source newsletter platform via Docker instead of a SaaS subscription.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Keila?
paragraphs: Keila is an open-source email newsletter platform released under the AGPLv3 license, offering visual and Markdown editors, contact segmentation, signup forms, and an API for transactional email.
It can be self-hosted for free via official Docker images, or used as Keila Cloud, a paid managed service hosted on European infrastructure, with tiers from €8/month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Keila is for
paragraphs: Keila is aimed at developers and businesses, particularly in Europe, who want an open-source or EU-hosted newsletter platform with privacy-focused analytics and no vendor lock-in.

enrichment.short_description: Sendy is a self-hosted, single-domain email newsletter script sold for a $69 one-time fee, designed to send via Amazon SES for low per-email cost.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sendy is a self-hosted email newsletter script with a $69 one-time license per domain, sending through Amazon SES for roughly $1 per 10,000 emails.

features.title: Drag-and-drop editor
features.description: WYSIWYG drag-and-drop email builder with an HTML editing option.

features.title: Campaign reporting
features.description: Analytics on opens, clicks, bounces, and geographic location of recipients.

features.title: AI Assistant (add-on)
features.description: Optional paid AI add-on for generating newsletters, subject lines, and campaign insights.

features.title: List segmentation
features.description: Segment subscriber lists to target specific groups.

features.title: Autoresponders
features.description: Automated drip email sequences.

features.title: White-labeled brands
features.description: Client account ('brand') management for agencies running multiple newsletters.

features.title: Amazon SES integration
features.description: Sends through Amazon SES, or SendGrid, Mailjet, and Elastic Email as alternatives, for roughly $1 per 10,000 emails.

features.title: Rules and webhooks
features.description: Automation triggers based on subscriber behavior.

pros: One-time $69 fee per domain instead of a recurring monthly SaaS subscription
Very low sending cost (about $1 per 10,000 emails) by routing through Amazon SES
Includes agency-friendly white-labeled client account management
Runs on standard PHP/MySQL hosting, similar to a WordPress install
cons: License is per-domain, so multi-domain use requires additional $69 licenses
Requires self-hosting on your own PHP/MySQL server and configuring Amazon SES yourself
AI Assistant is a separate paid add-on ($39-49), not included in the base license
No indication that the software is open source — it is a commercially licensed self-hosted script

faqs.question: Is Sendy a subscription?
faqs.answer: No, Sendy uses a one-time license fee ($69) per domain, with no recurring subscription.

faqs.question: How does Sendy keep sending costs low?
faqs.answer: It sends through Amazon SES, which costs roughly $1 per 10,000 emails.

faqs.question: What do I need to run Sendy?
faqs.answer: A standard web host with PHP and MySQL/MariaDB support.

faqs.question: Does Sendy include AI features?
faqs.answer: An AI Assistant is available as a separate paid add-on, not included in the base license.

use_cases.title: Low-cost self-hosted newsletters
use_cases.description: Send high-volume newsletters at a fraction of typical SaaS per-subscriber pricing by self-hosting and using Amazon SES.

use_cases.title: Agency newsletter management
use_cases.description: Manage separate white-labeled client newsletter accounts from one Sendy installation.

features: Sendy installed on your server by the Sendy team

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Sendy?
paragraphs: Sendy is a self-hosted email newsletter application sold as a one-time $69 license per domain rather than a monthly subscription. It sends email through Amazon SES (or alternatives like SendGrid, Mailjet, and Elastic Email) to keep per-email costs low.
It includes a drag-and-drop editor, campaign reporting, autoresponders, list segmentation, and white-labeled client 'brand' accounts, and runs on standard PHP/MySQL web hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sendy is for
paragraphs: Sendy is aimed at bloggers, marketers, and agencies who send large newsletter volumes and want to avoid recurring per-subscriber SaaS fees by self-hosting and paying Amazon SES sending costs directly.
